title: Cat Swallowed a Hair Tie: Emergency Guide & Triage Steps
meta_desc: If your cat swallowed a hair tie, it is a high-priority emergency. Learn the risks of intestinal blockage and why you should see a vet immediately.
content_html:
What Happens When a Cat Swallows a Hair Tie?

When a cat ingests a hair tie, it is considered a foreign body ingestion. Unlike food, the elastic and fabric materials of a hair tie cannot be broken down by digestive enzymes. These items often become trapped in the stomach or the narrow passages of the small intestine, leading to a physical obstruction that prevents food and water from passing through the digestive tract.

Is This a Veterinary Emergency?

The urgency level for hair tie ingestion is High. While some owners may hope the object passes naturally, hair ties are notorious for causing linear obstructions or clumping together with other ingested material like fur. If left untreated, a blockage can lead to intestinal perforation, tissue death, or severe dehydration. You should contact your veterinarian or an emergency animal hospital immediately, even if your cat is not yet showing symptoms.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

If you have a duplicate of the hair tie your cat swallowed, take a clear photo of it next to a common object like a coin for scale. Providing this photo to your veterinary team helps them assess the size, thickness, and material of the object. This information is vital for determining whether the item is likely to pass on its own or if immediate medical intervention, such as induced vomiting or endoscopic retrieval, is necessary.

faqs.question: What symptoms indicate a blockage?
faqs.answer: Common signs of a gastrointestinal blockage include repeated vomiting, loss of appetite, lethargy, abdominal tenderness, and an inability to keep down water.

faqs.question: Can I wait to see if my cat poops it out?
faqs.answer: Waiting is risky. Hair ties can remain in the stomach for days or weeks before moving into the intestines and causing a sudden, life-threatening emergency.

faqs.question: Should I induce vomiting at home?
faqs.answer: No. Never attempt to induce vomiting in a cat at home using hydrogen peroxide or other methods, as this can cause severe lung aspiration or esophageal damage.
